mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects peak capacity utilization and margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields positive 5-day forward returns in late-cycle expansions, contradicting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.3 percent forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signals collapsing construction demand and credit stress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in post-shock recovery periods consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward S&P 500 returns, invalidating the credit stress transmission channel.
